mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is near zero and CPIAUCSL is stable or falling, real discount rates decline and equity valuation multiples expand, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If triggered episodes with FEDFUNDS below 0.5% do not produce higher 20d SPX returns than non-triggered episodes, or if the signal turns negative for two consecutive rolling years, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in UNRATE reflects worsening household income prospects and tighter credit conditions, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 5 trading days when unemployment is climbing sharply.
failure_condition: Reject if monthly unemployment shock episodes are followed by positive 5d SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the average 5d return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized contraction in RSAFS and HOUST signals collapsing final demand and weak private sector confidence, which depresses S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60d SPX returns better than either series alone, or if the combined signal has IC near zero for any 3-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in INDPRO after a deep industrial slump marks inventory restocking and a cyclical production upswing, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change turns positive after a prior 6-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if positive turnarounds in INDPRO are followed by below-baseline or negative 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the effect disappears in recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll losses combined with rising unemployment identify a deep labor-market deterioration that erodes earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If joint payroll declines and unemployment increases do not outperform single-series versions in predicting negative 20d SPX returns, or if returns are positive in most such episodes, reject.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, an increase in FEDFUNDS above recent levels raises discount rates and compresses equity valuation, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if rate-hike episodes are followed by positive 60d SPX returns more often than negative ones, or if the average 60d return after tightening is not below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in RSAFS together with falling HOUST indicates weaker consumer spending and housing demand, so equity returns over the next 5 trading days turn negative when both indicators deteriorate simultaneously.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts do not increase the probability of negative 5d SPX returns relative to each series alone, or if the hit rate falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is rising while PAYEMS is still falling, firms are restoring output through productivity gains rather than broad labor recovery, and that mix supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not produce above-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if positive returns occur less than 55% of the time despite the trigger.
